reasoning: The evaluation identified two major defects: one from the LLM fidelity check (header text leaking into the middle of a continuous sentence, breaking reading order) and one programmatic defect (a bare URL not converted to a clickable hyperlink). Under the count-based routing rules, 1-5 major defects mandate a HUMAN_REVIEW outcome to correct the reading flow and ensure accessibility compliance for the link.

concerns.analysis: The running header 'FACT SHEET' from the top of page 2 is extracted as body content (a level 1 heading) and injected directly into the middle of a continuous sentence that spans across the page break. This breaks the reading flow between the words '...will be considered in' and 'the final permit decision.', constituting header leakage into the main body text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 ends with: 'All comments received during the public comment period or at the public hearings will be considered in'. Page 2 begins with the running header 'FACT SHEET', followed by the sentence continuation 'the final permit decision.'
concerns.output_quote: The JSON extracts the header: {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "FACT SHEET"} and places it right before {"type": "paragraph", "text": "the final permit decision..."}, interrupting the sentence started on page 1.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/laserfiche.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Page number artifacts retained: Page 1 of 2

Header/footer elements retained in output (1): Page 1 of 2
6/28/2023
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

action_items: Remove the leaked 'FACT SHEET' heading from the middle of the sentence bridging pages 1 and 2.
Convert the bare URL 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/laserfiche' into a proper clickable hyperlink.
Clean up any remaining header/footer artifacts like 'Page 1 of 2'.
